mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks such as text generation, audio processing, and image generation without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a local-first solution with a focus on privacy and control over their AI applications.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing LocalAI deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI to Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI with Helm.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall LocalAI from the specified namespace.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade LocalAI, including a backup step.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai -o json > localai-backup.json

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Backup current LocalAI pod configurations.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n localai -o json > localai-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the repository is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Review pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' and adjust configurations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the LocalAI service is running with 'kubectl get services -n localai'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image cannot be pulled due to authentication issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the image registry credentials are correctly set up.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved API endpoints for better performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy model formats; users should migrate to the new formats.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.11.x, ensure to update your model configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods in a 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources to run LocalAI.
